label: Asymptomatic elevated blood pressure without end-organ symptoms
inclusion_criteria: Adult with elevated blood pressure or asymptomatic hypertension documented at ED discharge.
Repeat blood pressure or measurement confirmation documented.
Explicit clinician documentation that no end-organ symptoms or hypertensive emergency concern are present.
No medication change, or clinician-entered medication plan documented.
exclusion_criteria: Blood pressure at hypertensive crisis threshold without individualized plan.
Any end-organ symptom such as chest pain, shortness of breath, neurologic symptoms, severe headache, vision change, confusion, decreased urination, hematuria, or tearing chest or back pain.
Stimulant or cocaine-associated hypertension, secondary hypertension concern, renal failure, dialysis/transplant pathway, or no reliable follow-up plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care or urgent care in the timeframe your clinician gave you to recheck blood pressure and adjust medicines if needed.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports hypertension education and hypertensive crisis threshold framing. AHA supports emergency escalation when very high blood pressure is accompanied by symptoms such as chest pain, dyspnea, back pain, weakness, vision change, or difficulty speaking. Clinician-owner review on 2026-06-10 approved this workflow-dependent narrow v1 because repeat BP confirmation, documented clinician assessment, concrete follow-up, and medication-plan documentation are required safeguards.

terms: primary care blood pressure follow up

repeat blood pressure or measurement confirmation documented
no end-organ symptoms or hypertensive emergency concern documented
no medication change or clinician-entered medication plan documented
